name: Mailchimp
what_it_does: Email marketing platform with audience management, campaign creation, and marketing automation, offering a REST API for programmatic list management, campaign sending, and analytics retrieval.
use_cases: Subscribing and unsubscribing users from mailing lists based on application events
Triggering transactional and marketing emails as part of automated user journeys
Syncing contact data and tags from CRM systems to keep audiences up to date
Retrieving campaign performance metrics (open rates, clicks) for reporting agents
Creating and scheduling email campaigns programmatically from template libraries
not_for: High-volume transactional email (use Mandrill/Mailchimp Transactional, Postmark, or SendGrid)
Real-time event-driven messaging requiring sub-second delivery
SMS or push notification channels (email only)
Organizations requiring HIPAA-compliant email (not covered under standard plan)
best_when: You're building marketing automation workflows, managing newsletter audiences, or syncing subscriber lists from your application and want a widely-adopted platform with a mature API.
avoid_when: You need primarily transactional email, very high send volumes, or advanced behavioral automation — dedicated transactional email or CDP platforms are more appropriate.

auth.notes: API keys provide full account access — no scope restriction per key. OAuth 2.0 is available for third-party app integrations. For agent workflows, use a dedicated API key and store it securely.

pricing.paid_tiers: Essentials from ~$13/month; Standard from ~$20/month; Premium from ~$350/month — scales with contact count
pricing.notes: Free tier is quite limited. Pricing scales significantly with list size. Mandrill (transactional) is separate and add-on only on paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: 10 simultaneous connections per API key; no documented per-minute limit but throttling occurs

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns standard HTTP status codes with detail field in JSON. Member already-subscribed errors return 400 with a specific title field that agents should check.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Subscriber add/update via PUT is idempotent using email hash as key. Campaign creation is not idempotent — check before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: List members are addressed by MD5 hash of lowercased email address — agents must hash emails before API calls
API keys have no scope restriction — treat as high-privilege credentials and never expose in client-side code
Audience IDs (list IDs) are required for almost every operation and differ per account/environment
Mailchimp silently deduplicates subscribers — adding existing subscriber updates rather than errors (usually desirable but can mask logic bugs)
Unsubscribing via API still keeps the contact in the audience as 'unsubscribed' — deletion is a separate operation
